experiment: A/B test: normal vs forced JSON structured output

results.finding: JSON mode helps. Structured output aligns with codec format, eliminates prose wrapping.
JSON mode hurts. Reasoning model loses expressiveness when forced into JSON.

results.json_mode: unsupported (LM Studio requires json_schema, not json_object)
results.finding: N/A — local runtime does not support json_object response format.

results.json_mode: unsupported (LM Studio requires json_schema, not json_object)
results.finding: Highest normal-mode score. 92.9% without structured output enforcement.
Strong improvement. JSON schema prevented T6 timeout, improved T1 (VIX) and T3 (urgency). Slight T2 regression (escalation detail dropped). Upgraded from S1-OPERATIONAL to S1-ADVANCED.
Slight regression. T1 calibration dropped (misinterpreted weight as share count in JSON mode). Already high-performing in normal mode.

conclusion: Structured output enforcement remains model-dependent. MedGemma 27B shows the strongest improvement (+15.8%), moving from OPERATIONAL to ADVANCED — the schema gives it output scaffolding that prevents timeouts and improves field extraction. LFM2-24B slightly degrades (-5.3%), similar to GLM-5 pattern: models already fluent in the codec gain nothing from double-constraining. DeepSeek-V3 benefits moderately (+7.2%). The §1 codec is inherently structured; JSON enforcement helps models that need output scaffolding but can hurt models that reason better in prose.
schema_note: LM Studio uses json_schema (strict) mode, not json_object. API providers (DeepSeek, GLM) used json_object mode. The two enforcement mechanisms are not identical.
